function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'printf' to reach function 'main'?
loop_reachability: Can the loop with condition 'i < N' be skipped entirely?
Is the loop with condition 'i < N' always executed?
Can the loop with condition 'j < N' be skipped entirely?
Is the loop with condition 'j < N' always executed?
dominators: Does the statement line 9:  <statement> if(i!=j && j!=k && i!=k )printf("%c%c%c\n",data[i],data[j],data[k]); </statement> dominate line 7:  <statement> for (int j = 0; j < N; ++j) </statement>?

Is it guaranteed that whenever line 8:  <statement> for (int k = 0; k < N; ++k) </statement> is reached, the statement line 5:  <statement> char data[6]={'0','1','2','3','4','5'}; </statement> has already been reached?
data_dependency: Does variable argc at line 3: <statement> int main(int argc, char const *argv[]) </statement> determine the value stored in variable k at line 8: <statement> 			for (int k = 0; k < N; ++k) </statement>?
liveness: Does variable 'k' remain in use at the end of function 'main'?
Is variable 'argc' live at the end of function 'main'?
dead_code: Can the program actually reach the statement: “for (int k = 0; k < N; ++k)”?
